DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: CROSS DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the arguments regarding the First Count for Wrongful Eviction. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as follows: (1) Cross-Complainant to state facts distinguishing the Third Count from the Sixth Count or remove one of either the Third Count or the Sixth Count from the Cross-Complaint as both counts are worded identically; and (2) Cross-Complainant to allege facts constituting a cause of action against Cross-Defendants regarding the claim of Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. If Cross-Complainant relies on allegations of fraudulent statements made by Cross-Defendants to support the claim of Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress, then facts should state the substance of these statements including who made them, when they were made, and how they contributed to the elements for a claim of Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Atty. RAY YETKA, Esq. of Utrecht & Lenvin, LLP (Tel: 415-357-0600) for plaintiffs;
Atty. DANIEL BERKO, Esq. (Tel: 415-771-6174) for defendant Laura Rios.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS SHLOMO FRANCO, SHOSHANA FRANCO, and YAFAH FRANCO's Motion For Order To (1) Compel Further Written Discovery And Documents (2) Deem Matters Admitted; And (3) Pay Monetary Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows: Motion is granted. Defendant is to respond to Special Interrogatories Nos. 3, 4, 5, 6, and 7. Defendant is to answer Request for Admissions Nos. 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 10, and 11. Defendant is produce documents as to Request for Production of Documents No. 46, and may redact all but the name and the claimed residence. Defendant is to produce documents as to Request for Production of Documents Nos. 47, 48, and 49, and may redact all but the children's claimed residence and the name of the school attended. Discovery is to be responded to/produced by October 1, 2021. No sanctions are granted as the dispute was a good faith dispute.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at greg@greggroeneveldlaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Greg Groeneveld; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
